slides.title: Design, Packaging, and Sustainability
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 2: Design, Packaging, and Sustainability. Welcome to Unit 2 of your Year 8 English course. In this unit, you will explore the fascinating world of design, packaging, and sustainability through the lens of a powerful story called "Signal in the Static." This story follows two courageous Somali students, Samira and Ibrahim, who run a youth radio station in Hargeisa and use their platform to investigate a secret land deal that threatens community gardens. Their journey will show you how design thinking, clear communication, and a commitment to truth can make a real difference in your community. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and analyse a narrative text, identifying themes of truth, bias, manipulation, and civic responsibility
Use partitive expressions correctly and confidently in both speaking and writing (e.g., a piece of evidence, a surge of indignation, a multitude of...
Form and use zero and first conditionals to express general truths and real future possibilities
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and analyse a narrative text, identifying themes of truth, bias, manipulation, and civic responsibility. you will also use partitive expressions correctly and confidently in both speaking and writing (e.g., a piece of evidence, a surge of indignation, a multitude of voices). you will also form and use zero and first conditionals to express general truths and real future possibilities. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: exult, loathe, usurp, feign, intervene, obstruct. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Design, Packaging, and Sustainability: source text 1
Design, Packaging, and Sustainability: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Design, Packaging, and Sustainability: source text 1 and Design, Packaging, and Sustainability: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Partitive Expressions
bullets: What Are Partitive Expressions? Partitive expressions help you talk about a portion or part of something. They make your sentences clearer and more...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Partitive Expressions. What Are Partitive Expressions? Partitive expressions help you talk about a portion or part of something. They make your sentences clearer and more specific. For example, instead of saying "I want cake," you can say "I want a piece of cake." Instead of saying "She felt happiness," you can say "She felt a surge of happiness." These small phrases make a big difference in how natural and precise your English sounds. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
